creator: romain@FreeBSD.org
text:
Thank you for the details, I though the puppetserver was a FreeBSD node and therefore the first node to try to fix. As I understand, the Linux nodes are behaving as expected, and the issue is only on FreeBSD after updating to Ruby 3.1.

I could reproduce your error by adding the archive module and an archive resource in my catalog.

While pushing my conf to the PuppetServer (also a FreeBSD in my case), r10k complained with:

remote: ERROR	 -> Command exited with non-zero exit code:
remote: Command: /usr/local/bin/puppet generate types --environment romain --environmentpath /usr/local/etc/puppet/code/environments --config /etc/puppetlabs/puppet/puppet.conf
remote: Stdout:
remote: Notice: Generating Puppet resource types.
remote: Stderr:
remote: /usr/local/etc/puppet/code/environments/romain/modules/archive/lib/puppet_x/bodeco/util.rb:67: warning: already initialized constant PuppetX::Bodeco::HTTP::FOLLOW_LIMIT
remote: /usr/local/etc/puppet/code/environments/romain/modules/archive/lib/puppet_x/bodeco/util.rb:67: warning: previous definition of FOLLOW_LIMIT was here
remote: /usr/local/etc/puppet/code/environments/romain/modules/archive/lib/puppet_x/bodeco/util.rb:68: warning: already initialized constant PuppetX::Bodeco::HTTP::URI_UNSAFE
remote: /usr/local/etc/puppet/code/environments/romain/modules/archive/lib/puppet_x/bodeco/util.rb:68: warning: previous definition of URI_UNSAFE was here
remote: Error: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/ruby: cannot load such file -- net/ftp
remote: Error: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/curl: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/ruby: cannot load such file -- net/ftp
remote: Error: Failed to load custom type 'archive' from '/usr/local/etc/puppet/code/environments/romain/modules/archive/lib/puppet/type/archive.rb': Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/curl: Could not autoload puppet/provider/archive/ruby: cannot load such file -- net/ftp
remote: Exit code: 1


Installing ftp/rubygem-net-ftp on the server fixed that issue on my PuppetServer.

Installing ftp/rubygem-net-ftp on the client fixed the issue you see \o/.

It seems that net/ftp was previously bundled in the ruby package and is not anymore.

The annoying thing is that if you want to tell puppet to install it, you have to do this in a working catalog and a catalog with an archive resource is de-facto broken until the package is installed.

Do you have some orchestration tools to install the package?  sysutils/bolt is currently broken (I will try to commit a work on and commit a workaround by the end of the week [1]), but ansible / choria and others can probably be leveraged to fix this issue.


Does this help?


References:
1. https://github.com/puppetlabs/orchestrator_client-ruby/issues/37
